allowed_abi_surfaces.detail: nullary i32-return entrypoints cover the bounded article demo and long-horizon kernels
direct scalar i32 arguments with one i32 return stay inside the declared article frontend/compiler envelope

disallowed_rows.title: C/C++ source ingress stays outside the declared article envelope
disallowed_rows.detail: the public article frontend/compiler envelope is Rust-source-only for now; the historical C-source compile receipt remains visible but out of envelope

disallowed_rows.title: std or alloc-backed Rust sources stay outside the declared article envelope
disallowed_rows.detail: the declared envelope admits `core`-only `#![no_std]` sources rather than a broader std/alloc source surface

disallowed_rows.title: wider numeric or multi-result ABI rows stay outside the declared article envelope
disallowed_rows.detail: the declared frontend/compiler envelope stays aligned with the already-bounded i32 article ABI rather than widening to i64 or multi-result rows

disallowed_rows.title: host imports and syscall-dependent rows stay outside the declared article envelope
disallowed_rows.detail: the declared envelope admits direct pure exports only; host calls, imported callbacks, and syscall-backed behavior remain out of scope

disallowed_rows.title: UB-dependent source rows stay outside the declared article envelope
disallowed_rows.detail: unsafe code is not rejected categorically, but rows whose semantics depend on undefined behavior stay outside the declared envelope

disallowed_rows.title: language revisions outside edition 2024 stay outside the declared article envelope
disallowed_rows.detail: public article-closure claims bind to the explicit current edition and toolchain policy instead of implying generic cross-edition frontend closure

manifest.current_truth_boundary: the declared article frontend/compiler envelope is one Rust-source-only `rustc` -> `wasm32-unknown-unknown` route over `#![no_std]` + `#![no_main]` sources, `core`-only source surface, `cdylib` output, `panic=abort`, and the already-bounded i32-oriented article ABI rows; C/C++, std/alloc surfaces, host imports, syscall-dependent rows, UB-dependent rows, and wider numeric or multi-result ABI rows stay outside this declared public envelope
non_implications: not arbitrary C or C++ article ingress
not host-import, syscall, or OS service closure
not a widening of the current bounded article ABI or interpreter breadth
manifest.claim_boundary: this manifest declares only the frontend/compiler envelope that current public article-closure claims are allowed to rely on. It does not by itself expand the corpus, prove article-demo parity, or turn final article-equivalence green.

claim_boundary: this report closes TAS-177 only. It expands the article-envelope frontend corpus beyond the old narrow canonical lane by compiling a broader committed Rust source set across arithmetic, branch-heavy, state-machine, allocator-backed memory, Hungarian-like, and Sudoku-like support families, while keeping typed refusal and toolchain-failure posture machine-readable. It does not yet claim article-demo frontend parity, arbitrary-program closure, or final article-equivalence green status.
summary: Article frontend corpus compile matrix now records tied_requirement_satisfied=true, compiled_cases=11/11, typed_refusals=4/4, toolchain_failures=1/1, category_coverage_green=true, envelope_alignment_green=true, compile_matrix_green=true, and article_equivalence_green=false.
